decisions.reasoning: Position down -14.55% from $425.31 entry. AI capex overhang ($190B/yr) is a genuine fundamental drag; OpenAI IPO delay news today (TheStreet, Jun 26) adds near-term pressure via MSFT OpenAI partnership uncertainty. However position is small ($1,090, 11.9% of equity) and July 29 earnings is a clear inflection catalyst where Azure growth data could reframe the capex-ROI narrative. Analyst consensus remains Strong Buy with avg. target $561-577 (MarketBeat). Selling at the lows locks in a loss without a recovery vehicle.

sources.headline: Microsofts $190 Billion AI Bet Is Both the Bull Case and Bear Case
Stock Market Today: Nasdaq falls on tech sell-off (OpenAI IPO delay)
MSFT analyst consensus Strong Buy, avg target $561-577

decisions.invalidation: Azure revenue growth decelerates materially at July 29 earnings, or management raises capex guidance without clear revenue offset

decisions.reasoning: Largest position at -10.74% from $217.09 entry ($2,907). Key medium-term catalysts intact: RTX Spark PC chip launching fall 2026 (CNBC May 31), Vera Rubin next-gen delivery H2 2026, CFO noting addressable market can double. 61/66 analysts rate Buy, price targets $245-300 vs $194 current (BofA/Citi). Near-term headwinds: chip price deflation per Kalshi derivatives traders (CNBC Jun 22) and $25B bond offering adding leverage. SMA flat but position thesis remains intact.

sources.headline: Nvidias new RTX Spark chip to power Windows laptops from Dell, HP, Lenovo (fall 2026)
Nvidias stock struggles as Kalshi traders bet chip prices are coming down
Bank of America names NVDA top pick for June, target $300

decisions.invalidation: Vera Rubin delivery delayed beyond H2 2026, or US export controls expand to further restrict high-end GPU sales, or chip ASP decline accelerates beyond 10% QoQ

decisions.reasoning: Only long SMA signal in portfolio (fast $724.65 > slow $722.46). Broad tech ETF, down -4.27% from $742.88 entry. Today Nasdaq falls 1.25% on AI narrative concerns. Holding for broad tech recovery; best technical posture in the portfolio.

sources.headline: Nasdaq down 1.25% on AI infrastructure cost concerns

decisions.invalidation: QQQ SMA flips to bearish (fast crosses below slow) or broad tech sell-off deepens beyond -5% week

decisions.reasoning: Nearly flat (-0.14% from $733.31 entry). S&P 500 ex-tech seeing sector rotation into industrials/energy/healthcare today. Anchor position. FOMC held rates at June 16-17 meeting (4th consecutive hold); PCE at 3.8% headline (Kraken Blog Jun 24) remains elevated but jobs strong (+172K May). Broad market base.

sources.headline: PCE data and June jobs report headline a data-heavy two weeks ahead
Six of 11 S&P 500 sectors rising; rotation into industrials and healthcare

decisions.invalidation: PCE re-accelerates forcing FOMC to resume hikes, or consumer sentiment collapse drives recession fears

summary: First session: held all four inherited positions (MSFT, NVDA, QQQ, SPY); no trades on tech selloff day driven by OpenAI IPO delay concerns; watching MSFT July 29 earnings and NVDA Vera Rubin H2 delivery as key catalysts
open_theses: MSFT: hold through July 29 earnings — Azure growth data will clarify whether $190B AI capex generates sufficient returns; exit thesis if Azure decelerates
NVDA: hold for RTX Spark PC chip launch (fall 2026) and Vera Rubin delivery; largest position, monitor chip price deflation data
QQQ: only long SMA signal; hold as broad tech exposure anchor
SPY: near-flat anchor, hold as broad market hedge; sector rotation supports ex-tech names
